Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Actually the supply chain: SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS. So any later activity can follow any earlier after some steps. Thus we can include pairs for any earlier to later activity in the chain. e.g., (SS, OP), (SS, RC), (SS, QI), (SS, CA), (SS, PT), (SS, PK), (SS, WS), (SS, DT), (SS, AS); then (OP, RC), (OP, QI), ... up to (DT, AS). Also possibly reverse? Probably not; time flows forward.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to assign average time and standard deviation for each pair based on typical durations and variability. Lets assign each step a mean time and maybe a coefficient of variation. We can define base times for each activity: SS: 0.5 day (43200 sec) OP: 0.3 day (25920 sec) RC: 0.2 day (17280 sec) QI: 0.15 day (12960 sec) CA: 0.4 day (34560 sec) PT: 0.25 day (21600 sec) PK: 0.15 day (12960 sec) WS: 0.1 day (8640 sec) DT: 0.05 day (4320 sec) AS: 0.02 day (1728 sec)

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But these are just durations of each activity itself, not the time between activities. Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Well define a function to compute average time as sum of mean durations of both activities? For pair (X, Y): avg = mean_time[X] + mean_time[Y]; std = sqrt(std_time[X]^2 + std_time[Y]^2) maybe. We need assign std times for each activity as well. Lets assign std times (maybe 20% of mean). So: SS: mean 43200, std 8640 OP: mean 25920, std 5184 ...

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now compute for each pair. 1. (SS,OP): mean = 43200+25920 = 69120 sec; std = sqrt(8640^2+5184^2) = 10076 sec ... 9. (DT,AS): mean =4320+1728=6048 sec; std = 931 sec.
